faq.desc: Quickly find answers to your questions about car rental in Morocco

i1.q: What documents are needed to rent a car?
i1.a: ID card or passport, valid driver's license

i2.q: What is the minimum age to rent a car?
i2.a: The minimum age is 21. Some models may require 23 or older.

i3.q: How long must I have held my license?
i3.a: A license valid for at least 2 years is mandatory.

i4.q: Do I have to pay a deposit?
i4.a: Yes, a deposit may be required depending on the chosen model. It is returned after the vehicle is returned.

i5.a: Yes, delivery and pickup at Rabat-Salé Airport are available on request.

i7.q: What payment methods do you accept?

i8.q: Can I add a second driver?
i8.a: Yes, an additional driver is possible upon presentation of their license.

i9.q: What to do in case of breakdown or accident?
i9.a: Contact us immediately. 24/7 assistance included with each rental.

i10.a: Yes, all our cars are insured with all-risk insurance and a 5% deductible.

r1.text: I rented from them last month, clean service and new car. May God bless you.

r2.text: Fantastic service. The car was delivered to my hotel on time and was impeccable. Simple and efficient.

r3.text: Reasonable price and very professional staff. I highly recommend for your trips to Morocco.

r4.text: Finally a rental agency where 'new car' actually means new. The Duster ran perfectly and the AC was a lifesaver. Quick drop-off at the airport!

r5.text: Loyal customer for 6 months. It's rare to find such seriousness in Sala Al Jadida. Neat cars and fixed prices, even in high season. Well done!

r6.text: Very good value for money. I was a bit afraid at first but the deposit was returned immediately after the return. Serious.
